updates.title: When AI Builds Itself — Anthropic Reveals Claude Writes 80%+ of Its Own Code, Calls for Global AI Pause Option
updates.summary: Anthropic's Institute published research disclosing that Claude now authors more than 80% of the code merged into Anthropic's production codebase — up from low single digits before Claude Code launched in early 2025 — with engineers shipping roughly 8× more code per quarter. The paper warns this trajectory could lead to recursive self-improvement, the threshold where AI designs and builds its own successors with minimal human input. Anthropic calls for maintaining a global option to slow or temporarily pause frontier AI development, stating the inflection point 'could come sooner than most institutions are prepared for.'
updates.accuracy_notes: Published directly by Anthropic Institute; independently confirmed by Fortune (June 5, 2026), Tom's Hardware, Scientific American, The Next Web, VentureBeat, and Winbuzzer (URL contains '2026/06/05'). The 80% figure is Anthropic's own self-reported metric from internal telemetry. The recursive self-improvement warning is a policy position, not a claim of current capability — reviewed coverage handles this distinction accurately. No red flags.

updates.title: Anthropic's Claude Services Down — claude.ai, Claude Code, and Cowork Affected; Data Leak Claims Under Investigation
updates.summary: On June 5, 2026, Anthropic's Claude platform suffered a significant multi-service outage beginning at 8:08 PT (15:08 UTC), impacting claude.ai, the Claude API, Claude Code, and Claude Cowork. Recovery was staggered by model — Opus 4.6 restored first, Opus 4.8 last — with full service confirmed back by 18:27 UTC. Anthropic attributed the incident to an infrastructure issue (not a model bug). A separate report surfaced customer data leak claims, which Anthropic stated it is actively investigating with no confirmed exposure as of reporting time.
updates.accuracy_notes: Outage timeline corroborated by Anthropic's public status page (status.claude.com) and multiple independent outlets including TechRadar, ibtimes.com.au, and sundayguardianlive.com. The customer data leak claim is unconfirmed — Anthropic stated 'no evidence' but investigation is ongoing; reviewed coverage is appropriately cautious. Note: this story was not directly confirmed on XDA-Developers or The Verge within the monitored June 5-7 window; sourced from secondary coverage as XDA/Verge are inaccessible.

updates.title: Anthropic Platform Release Notes — June 5, 2026: Opus 4.1 Deprecation, Advisor Tool Public Beta, AWS Managed Agents GA
updates.summary: Anthropic's June 5 platform release notes contain three developer-facing updates: (1) Claude Opus 4.1 is being deprecated from the Claude API with retirement scheduled for August 5, 2026, requiring developer migration; (2) the advisor tool enters public beta, pairing a fast executor model with a higher-intelligence advisor model for long-horizon agentic workloads; (3) Claude Managed Agents webhooks, multiagent orchestration, and self-hosted sandboxes are now generally available on Claude Platform on AWS, expanding enterprise deployment options.
updates.accuracy_notes: Confirmed via Google search index snippets of docs.anthropic.com/en/release-notes/overview (direct page access returned HTTP 403). Content is consistent with Anthropic's established model deprecation pattern and the previously announced AWS partnership. Managed Agents and the advisor tool referenced by multiple secondary sources. No red flags.
